name: From Atienza to Marquezado de Berlanga — circular via the Castillo de Atienza
name_original: De Atienza al Marquezado de Berlanga — circular por el Castillo de Atienza
region_intro: Road cycling around Atienza offers diverse terrain, making it suitable for various fitness levels. The region features mostly paved surfaces, ideal for road biking, traversing through rugged, pine-covered mountains and deep ravines. Cyclists can explore routes that pass by historic landmarks and natural features, providing both physical challenge and scenic exploration. Best road cycling routes around Atienza The most popular road cycling route is From Atienza to Marquezado de Berlanga — circular via the Castillo de Atienza, a 49.7 miles (80.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and passes by the historic Castle of Atienza. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Pela y Sierra de Bulejo - Archaeological Site of Tiermes — circular from Atienza, a difficult 62.2 miles (100.1 km) path. This route explores mountainous terrain and includes the Archaeological Site of Tiermes. Local road cyclists also love the From Medranda to Fuencemillán — circular from Pinilla de Jadraque, a 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Road cycling around Atienza is defined by historic towns, pine-covered mountains, and deep ravines. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging ability levels. The routes in Atienza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 160 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Atienza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Atienza?
A: Atienza offers a network of over a dozen road cycling routes, providing diverse options for exploring the region. These routes traverse varied landscapes, from rugged mountains to picturesque fields.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Atienza?
A: The road cycling routes around Atienza feature mostly paved surfaces, ideal for road biking. You'll encounter diverse terrain, including rugged, pine-covered mountains and deep ravines, offering both challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for different fitness levels in Atienza?
A: Yes, Atienza caters to various fitness levels. While many routes are considered difficult, requiring very good fitness, there are also several moderate options available. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375734695/from-medranda-to-fuencemillan-circular-from-pinilla-de-jadraque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Medranda to Fuencemillán — circular from Pinilla de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, while routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017643020/from-atienza-to-marquezado-de-berlanga-circular-via-the-castillo-de-atienza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Atienza to Marquezado de Berlanga — circular via the Castillo de Atienza&lt;/a&gt; present a more difficult challenge.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while cycling around Atienza?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical landmarks. You can encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Atienza&lt;/a&gt;, a fortress with Muslim origins, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4442873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Highlights of Atienza Village&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sites include the Roman Fountain by San Bartolomé Church and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2677132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rello Walled Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts diverse natural beauty. Cyclists can pass by the tranquil Embalse del río Cañamares (Cañamares River Reservoir), the unique Salinas de Imón (salt flats), or even the scenic Cascada de Lumías (Lumías Waterfall). You might also cycle through vibrant Campos de Trigo y Amapolas (wheat and poppy fields) depending on the season, and enjoy views from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Carrascosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Atienza?
A: The road cycling experience in Atienza is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the combination of challenging routes through pine-covered mountains and deep ravines, alongside the opportunity to discover historic towns and natural features.

Q: What is a popular challenging road cycling route in the Atienza area?
A: A popular challenging route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017225160/sierra-de-pela-y-sierra-de-bulejo-archaeological-site-of-tiermes-circular-from-atienza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Pela y Sierra de Bulejo - Archaeological Site of Tiermes — circular from Atienza&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 100 km path explores mountainous terrain and includes significant elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Atienza?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Atienza are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Atienza Castle loop from Pálmaces de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views and a complete loop.

Q: Can I find routes that feature specific historical or industrial heritage?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses into the region's rich history and industry. You might pass by the Prádena de Atienza Stone Houses, showcasing traditional architecture, or areas related to the Hiendelaencina Mining Heritage, providing a unique cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Atienza?
A: While specific weather patterns vary, the region's diverse landscapes are enjoyable across many seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with wheat and poppy fields adding color in spring. Summer can be warm, while winter might present cooler conditions, especially in the higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging ride?
A: For cyclists seeking significant elevation gain, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017172610/ascent-to-el-picoz-round-trip-from-hiendelaencina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Picoz — round trip from Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt; is a notable option. This difficult route features over 1000 meters of ascent, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience through the mountainous terrain.
tips: Town hidden in the narrow spaces of a gorge traced by the Talegones River. With good examples of traditional architecture in its houses, the parish church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción preserves Romanesque elements, although it was heavily reformed.

Climbing up from Berlanga de Duero, I gave my all, I argued with myself, but I won. If you are carrying panniers, grit your teeth and put on the smallest chainring and the largest sprocket and go ahead.
Romanillos de Atienza is a small town in the north of the province next to the Camino del Cid. There is a water fountain next to the church to fill your bottles and cool off.

The Castillo de Atienza, like so many fortifications in the border area between the Moors and the Spaniards, was in rather unstable ownership. From the 9th to the 11th century it was part of the Moorish defense lines south of the Duero and was considered an almost impregnable fortress.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption
